resources.string: Adds haptic feedback to taps system-wide.

Hapticks listens for tap accessibility events so it can trigger the device vibrator. It does not read, store, or transmit any on-screen content. You can turn it off at any time from Settings > Accessibility.
Control how taps feel across your device
Pick the texture of each tap
Enable vibration feedback for every tap
Gentle tap like hitting a soft wall
Hapticks needs the accessibility service to fire haptic feedback when you tap in any app. Your content is never read or stored.
Adds responsive touch feedback across apps and system UI for a more refined, premium feel
Feel a firm pulse the instant a scroll hits the absolute top or bottom in any app
More haptic experiences are on the way. Stay tuned.
Hooks RecyclerView, ScrollView, NestedScrollView, and WebView so you feel exactly when a list cannot scroll any further.
Fires a distinct pulse the instant you hit the top or bottom of any scrollable surface
The hook is live in this process. Scope the module to more apps from LSPosed Manager to extend coverage.
Edge Haptics relies on LSPosed to hook into other apps\' scroll containers. Install a Magisk-based root and LSPosed to enable this feature.
Root is present but LSPosed has not injected this app. Open LSPosed Manager, enable the \u201cHapticks\u201d module, add Hapticks to its scope, then restart.
Tip: add other apps to the Hapticks module scope in LSPosed Manager to feel edge haptics inside them.
Hapticks Edge Haptics: pixel-perfect top/bottom scroll feedback for RecyclerView, ScrollView, NestedScrollView, and WebView.
